medications: Gabapentin 300 mg at bedtime (as needed for nerve pain)
Naproxen 500 mg as needed for back pain (with food)
health.mental_health: Ongoing stress and low mood related to immigrating, language barriers at appointments, and worry about maintaining steady work while managing chronic conditions; occasional insomnia when finances are tight.
recent_visits: Primary care visit to establish care and review blood pressure/diabetes after moving (last month)
A1C and lipid panel labs at Atrium Health outpatient lab (3 weeks ago)
Physical therapy evaluation for chronic low back pain (2 months ago)
Eye exam for diabetic retinopathy screening at a local optometry clinic (4 months ago)

finance.debt: Medical bills from initial U.S. clinic visits and labs; small personal loan used to cover immigration-related fees and move-in costs; carrying a balance on a secured credit card while building U.S. credit history.
investments: Cash savings in a credit union account
recent_transactions: Wire transfer/remittance to family abroad for support
Payment plan set up with a clinic for lab and visit charges
Purchase of used hand tools and a diagnostic scanner for work
Deposit and first month’s rent for an apartment in east Charlotte

relationships: Partner: Marisol Herrera (still abroad; daily calls and planning her move)
Adult daughter: Daniela Herrera (in another state; helps him navigate paperwork and insurance)
Cousin: Rafael Herrera (in Charlotte; occasional rides and help finding housing)
Neighbor: Mrs. Evelyn Carter (checks in and shares local tips; friendly but not close)
Church contact: Pastor Luis Molina (Spanish-language congregation; social support)
social.social_media_activity: Uses WhatsApp heavily for family and work coordination; has a Facebook account mostly for community groups (Charlotte buy/sell and Spanish-speaking resources) and rarely posts personal updates.
private_conversations: Discussing immigration paperwork timelines and fear of making a mistake on forms
Talking with his partner about money stress and how much he can send home
Asking his daughter for help understanding medical insurance terms and bills
Discussing back pain limitations and worry about job security if he can’t keep up physically

schedule.work_hours: Mon–Fri 8:00 AM–5:30 PM; occasional Saturday 9:00 AM–1:00 PM when the shop is busy
regular_appointments: Primary care follow-up every 3 months for blood pressure and diabetes
Physical therapy exercises at home 4–5 evenings per week; in-clinic PT as needed
Saturday morning church service twice a month
upcoming_events: DMV appointment to update driver’s license documentation (next week)
Diabetes nutrition education class at a community clinic (in 2 weeks)
Follow-up appointment to review A1C results and adjust medications (next month)
Video call with an immigration advisor at a local nonprofit (in 10 days)
schedule.daily_routine: Up around 6:00 AM to check blood sugar, take morning meds, and pack a simple lunch; works on his feet most of the day; short walk after dinner when back pain allows; evening calls to family abroad and reviewing paperwork/English practice.

professional.company: Queen City Auto Care (independent repair shop in Charlotte)
projects: Diagnosing intermittent misfire and fuel trim issues on late-model Ford and Chevy vehicles using scan tool data
Rebuilding front suspension components on high-mileage SUVs (ball joints, control arms, alignment prep)
Learning the shop’s point-of-sale system and parts ordering workflow
Preparing for North Carolina state inspection procedures and certification requirements
professional.performance_issues: Occasional slowdowns due to back pain during heavy lifts; sometimes asks a coworker to double-check estimates because he’s still adjusting to technical terms in English and doesn’t want to misquote customers. He’s concerned that missed hours for medical appointments could affect his standing.
workplace_relationships: Shop owner: Greg Whitaker (supportive but expects speed and clean comebacks)
Service writer: Tasha Nguyen (helpful with customer communication; sometimes impatient when the shop is slammed)
Senior tech: Mike O’Donnell (mentors him on local inspection norms; trades shifts when James has appointments)

hobbies: Fixing up an older pickup truck on weekends when he has time
Watching soccer and following Liga MX/MLS
Fishing at local lakes when invited
Cooking familiar meals from home and trying Charlotte food trucks
habits: Checks blood sugar most mornings and keeps a log on his phone
Limits sugary drinks but still craves sweet bread when stressed
Uses over-the-counter topical pain relief and heat packs after long shifts
Occasionally drinks 1–2 beers on weekends; avoids it when blood pressure is high
Practices English with a phone app at night and keeps a small notebook of automotive vocabulary
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a modest one-bedroom apartment in east Charlotte; keeps costs low and shares some furniture and household items with his cousin until he’s fully set up. Most of his family is still abroad, and he’s planning for his partner to join him when paperwork and finances allow.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Trying to follow a lower-sodium, lower-carb diet for hypertension and diabetes—more grilled chicken, beans, vegetables, and smaller portions of rice/tortillas; struggles when working late and relying on quick takeout.
